claim: Hybrid search combining keyword and semantic search provides the best accuracy for both exact term matching and conceptual understanding in RAG systems
actionable: Implement hybrid search by running both semantic (vector) and keyword (fuzzy text) searches in parallel, then merge results using reciprocal rank fusion to normalize scores across different scales
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Semantic search struggles with specific values like years (e.g., finding 2025 revenue instead of 2023) because it's concept-based rather than exact-matching
actionable: When your RAG queries need exact year/date/number matching, bias toward keyword search or hybrid search in the system prompt rather than pure semantic search
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Reciprocal rank fusion (RRF) is the algorithm to merge semantic and keyword search results by converting scores to rank positions, normalizing across different score scales
actionable: Convert raw similarity scores from semantic search (0-1 range) and text search (unbounded integers) into rank positions, then apply RRF formula to create normalized combined scores
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Fuzzy matching in keyword search allows typos and prefix variations, making text search more flexible and preventing failures from misspellings
actionable: Enable fuzzy matching with configurable edit distance and prefix length in your text search implementation to tolerate agent-generated spelling variations
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: MongoDB can efficiently serve as both a vector database and NoSQL database for RAG, storing both embeddings and document chunks with semantic and text search capabilities
actionable: Use MongoDB with vector search indexes and text search indexes to implement hybrid RAG without requiring a separate vector database
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Docling is a Python library that extracts text from PDFs, Word docs, markdown, and audio files while using hybrid chunking strategy for better semantic boundaries
actionable: Replace basic text splitting with Docling + hybrid chunking to automatically identify semantic boundaries in documents, improving RAG retrieval quality
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Pydantic AI is a lightweight framework for building AI agents with flexibility and good documentation that integrates well with tool definitions
actionable: Use Pydantic AI as your agent framework when you need type-safe tool definitions and prefer a lighter alternative to heavier orchestration frameworks
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Hybrid RAG with proper aggregation pipelines can complete multi-stage searches in under 1 second despite combining semantic and keyword searches
actionable: When implementing hybrid RAG, ensure your aggregation pipeline stages are optimized (vector lookup, document join, unwind, score extraction) to keep latency under 1 second
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Agents should be prompted to choose between keyword, semantic, or hybrid search based on query type; this agentic RAG approach is more flexible than fixed search strategies
actionable: Give agents a system prompt with clear guidelines: use keyword search for specific terms/dates, semantic search for conceptual similarity, hybrid for mixed queries
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)

claim: Metadata from document records (source file, ingestion date, file length) should be joined with chunks during retrieval so agents can cite sources and understand context
actionable: In your RAG pipeline aggregation, join chunk results with document metadata collections to provide agents with source attribution and contextual information
source_title: The Simplest RAG Stack That Actually Works (Complete Guide)
